content.problemStatement:
Chef gives you an array A=[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč] of length NNN.

Let f(S)f(S)f(S) denote the bitwise OR of a non-empty subset SSS of AAA.

Find the maximum possible value of f(S1)‚äēf(S2)f(S_1) \oplus f(S_2)f(S1‚Äč)‚äēf(S2‚Äč) across all ways to **partition** the elements of AAA into two non-empty subsets S1S_1S1‚Äč and S2S_2S2‚Äč.

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operator.

Note that you must partition AAA, i.e, every element of AAA must belong to either S1S_1S1‚Äč or S2S_2S2‚Äč.


Input Format

First line of the input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ  the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print the maximum possible value of f(S1)‚äēf(S2)f(S_1) \oplus f(S_2)f(S1‚Äč)‚äēf(S2‚Äč) across all partitions of AAA into S1S_1S1‚Äč and S2S_2S2‚Äč.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The given array is [1,3,7,15][ 1 , 3 , 7 , 15 ][1,3,7,15].
Choose S1={1}S_1 = \{1\}S1‚Äč={1} and S2={3,7,15}S_2 = \{3, 7, 15\}S2‚Äč={3,7,15} to obtain f(S1)=1f(S_1) = 1f(S1‚Äč)=1 and f(S2)=3‚ą£7‚ą£15=15f(S_2) = 3\mid 7\mid 15 = 15f(S2‚Äč)=3‚ą£7‚ą£15=15, giving us f(S1)‚äēf(S2)=14f(S_1) \oplus f(S_2) = 14f(S1‚Äč)‚äēf(S2‚Äč)=14.
You may verify that no other partition gives a larger value.
Test case 222: The given array is [1,1,2,2][ 1 , 1 ,2 , 2 ][1,1,2,2].
Choose S1={1,1}S_1 = \{1, 1\}S1‚Äč={1,1} and S2={2,2}S_2 = \{2, 2\}S2‚Äč={2,2} to obtain f(S1)‚äēf(S2)=3f(S_1) \oplus f(S_2) = 3f(S1‚Äč)‚äēf(S2‚Äč)=3, which is the maximum possible.
Test case 333: The given array is [3,4][ 3 , 4 ][3,4].
We have no choice: any partition will give us 3‚äē4=73\oplus 4 = 73‚äē4=7.
